name: CrowdSec Engine
description: Open-source intrusion detection: parses logs, detects attack patterns (brute force, port scans, bot scraping), and publishes decisions for bouncers to enforce.
author: Crow

install_consent_messages.en: CrowdSec requires read-only access to the Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) so it can tail logs from every container on this host for attack-pattern detection. Crow enforces read-only Docker socket (no writes, no container control). CrowdSec does NOT enforce decisions by itself — it only detects and records. To actually block attackers you install a separate 'bouncer' (firewall-bouncer, nginx-bouncer, etc.); the MVP ships detection only. See the skill for the follow-up PR that adds a firewall bouncer.
install_consent_messages.es: CrowdSec necesita acceso de solo lectura al socket de Docker (/var/run/docker.sock) para leer logs de cada contenedor en este host y detectar patrones de ataque. Crow impone solo lectura (sin escrituras ni control de contenedores). CrowdSec NO aplica decisiones por sí mismo — solo detecta y registra. Para bloquear atacantes instalas un 'bouncer' aparte (firewall-bouncer, nginx-bouncer, etc.); el MVP incluye solo detección. El skill documenta el PR siguiente con el bouncer de firewall.

env_vars.name: CROWDSEC_LAPI_URL
env_vars.description: Local API URL (reachable from host for bouncers)

env_vars.name: CROWDSEC_API_KEY
env_vars.description: Bouncer API key (auto-generated on first boot; read from cscli bouncers list)

notes: Detection only — no enforcement. Bouncer bundles (PR 4.5+) attach to the LAPI on 127.0.0.1:8091 and enforce decisions. Logs from every running container are tailed via Docker socket (read-only). Default collections: linux, base-http-scenarios, whitelists. Console enrollment optional (cscli console enroll <token>).
